topic: Unit 2: Listening, Reading and Use of French
subject: french

items.why: Students assume librairie = library. It actually means BOOKSHOP. The French word for library is bibliothèque. Both relate to books but serve different purposes.

items.q: When does the past participle agree with avoir verbs?
items.correct: When the direct object comes BEFORE the verb (la pomme que j'ai mangée).
items.why: Students learn 'avoir = no agreement' as a blanket rule. The exception is PRECEDING DIRECT OBJECT: 'Je l'ai mangée' (la pomme) requires the participle to agree with the object pronoun.
Sensible is a faux ami. French 'sensible' = sensitive/easily affected. English 'sensible' = raisonnable in French. Confusion leads to wrong impressions of someone's character.

items.q: Who directed À bout de souffle (1960)?
items.why: Correct answer: Godard. “Truffaut” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: passé simple. “passé composé” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was Les Quatre Cents Coups released?
items.why: The real figure is 1959. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
